name: State of Vermont Employee Salaries

columns.name: Job Type
columns.description: Classified, Exempt, General Assembly, Temporary, or Contractual

description: Salary information for State of Vermont active employees from all branches. Generally, employees who are expected to work year-round have salaries stated as salary type "Annual." Temporary employees have salaries stated as salary type "Hourly." Legislators, who usually do not work year-round, have salaries stated as salary type "Weekly." The weekly rate reported here is the rate paid during the legislative session. Data as of the last Friday of each month. NOTE: Individuals covered by the Safe a

name: State of Vermont Grants Issued
description: Beginning January 31, 2010, pursuant to 32 V.S.A. § 314, the Department of Finance and Management is required to issue an annual report of all grants awarded. In 2012, the Department began a pilot program to include non-federal grants in its database; this effort continues today, and those grants are included in the dataset. Awards included in this dataset reflect grants authorized by a State of Vermont department to a grantee during the applicable report year. Funds may or may not have been exp

columns.name: Total Expenses
columns.description: Includes reimbursement for mileage, meals and other expenses.

columns.name: Total Compensation
columns.description: The sum of "Total Pay," and "Total Benefits."

columns.name: Other Pay
columns.description: This is a category of payments made to the employee that are not "pay" or "overtime" and includes such items as shift differential, standby, clothing allowance, annual leave payoff (upon retirement or RIF), prior year compensatory leave payoff, etc.

columns.name: Job Type
columns.description: Classified, Exempt, General Assembly, Temporary, or Contractual

columns.name: Overtime Pay
columns.description: This is cash paid for hours worked in excess of defined workday and/or workweek at rates established by law and/or in the applicable Collective Bargaining Agreements.

columns.name: Total Benefits
columns.description: State contribution to Retirement and Social Security, and where applicable Health, Dental, Life, and Long Term Disability Insurance,  and Employee Assistance Program.

columns.name: Total Pay
columns.description: The sum of "Pay," "Other Pay," and "Overtime Pay."

columns.name: Overtime Units
columns.description: Units used to calculate overtime pay are based on premium and straight-time hours worked, shift differential and the like.  Note: Overtime units cannot the used to determine overtime hours worked.

columns.name: Pay
columns.description: The amount paid to the employee for "standard hours", which for most full-time employees is 2,080 hours per year (some protective service employees have standard hours greater than 2,080).  This includes "regular" pay, paid holidays, sick leave, annual leave, compensatory leave, personal leave,  and paid leave.  Historically, temporary employees were paid only for hours worked - however, as of 2017 temporary employees are also paid for sick leave per 21 V.S.A. § 487. General Assembly members are paid a weekly rate during the legislative session.

columns.name: Multiple Record
columns.description: When "Multiple Record" is indicated this means that there is more than one record for the same employee.  This can be the result of that employee changing jobs, being employed in multiple temporary jobs, or moving from one job type to another (e.g., moving from a temporary job to a classified job).

description: State employee salary, benefits and expense reimbursement information starting with fiscal year 2015. For the purpose of this report, all employees of the Department of States Attorneys and Sheriffs are included. Employees in the respective county offices , such as States Attorneys, Sheriffs and Deputy States Attorneys are County employees not State employees. name: Vermont Child Care Provider Data
provider: Department for Children and Families (DCF), Child Development Division

columns.name: Current STARS Level
columns.description: The provider's current level (1-5 STARS) on the State of Vermont's QRIS scale.

columns.name: Reported Food Program Participation
columns.description: Whether a provider has reported participating in the Child and Adult Care Food Program.

columns.name: Prequalified Prekindgarten End Date
columns.description: The date the program must renew their prequalified prekindergarten status.

columns.name: Special Services Status
columns.description: Whether a provider is approved to offer Specialized Services.

columns.name: Special Schedule
columns.description: Any days/times program operates outside of the typical schedule.

columns.name: File Name
columns.description: Name of the working file. Used in part to track reporting period.

columns.name: Infant Licensed Capacity
columns.description: The maximum number of infants a provider could theoretically serve at one time, given sufficient staffing, subject to applicable regulations.

columns.name: Reported Infant Vacancies
columns.description: How many infants a provider with an active referral agreement reportedly had room for when their referral agreement was last updated.

columns.name: Provider Referral Status
columns.description: Whether a provider has an active referral agreement or not.

columns.name: Toddler Licensed Capacity
columns.description: The maximum number of toddlers a provider could theoretically serve at one time, given sufficient staffing, subject to applicable regulations.

columns.name: School Age Licensed Capacity
columns.description: The maximum number of school age children a provider could theoretically serve at one time, given sufficient staffing, subject to applicable regulations.

columns.name: Accreditation Start Date
columns.description: When a provider's current accreditation began.

columns.name: Accreditation End Date
columns.description: When a provider's current accreditation expires.

columns.name: Prequalified Prekindgarten Program
columns.description: Prequalified Prekindergarten Programs are qualified for payment for public preschool attendance under VT Act 166.

columns.name: Date Capacity and Vacancies Updated
columns.description: The date that the program reported the capacity and vacancies.

columns.name: Provider ID
columns.description: The provider's "Person Record" in BFIS, containing links to their Credential Case (if an individual), Grant Case (if applicable), and Provider Case.

columns.name: Provider Case
columns.description: The ID number for the folder that holds the licensing history for each individual provider.

columns.name: License ID
columns.description: The distinct license number for each individual provider.

columns.name: Current License Start Date
columns.description: When the current license term began.

columns.name: Current License End Date
columns.description: When the current license term expires.

columns.name: License Type
columns.description: Whether the provider is Licensed (afterschool programs, center-based programs, license family homes) or Registered (registered family homes). Different CCFAP rates apply depending on whether the program is Licensed or Registered.

columns.name: Provider Program Type
columns.description: What specific type of program the provider is.

columns.name: AHS District
columns.description: The three-letter abbreviation for which Agency of Human Services district office jurisdiction the provider's town is in.

columns.name: Total Licensed Capacity
columns.description: The maximum number of children a provider could theoretically serve at one time, given sufficient staffing, subject to applicable regulations.

columns.name: Preschool Licensed Capacity
columns.description: The maximum number of preschoolers a provider could theoretically serve at one time, given sufficient staffing, subject to applicable regulations.

columns.name: Accreditation
columns.description: What type of accreditation (e.g. NAEYC) a provider has.

columns.name: Reported Toddler Vacancies
columns.description: How many toddlers a provider with an active referral agreement reportedly had room for when their referral agreement was last updated.

columns.name: Reported Preschool Vacancies
columns.description: How many preschoolers a provider with an active referral agreement reportedly had room for when their referral agreement was last updated.

columns.name: Reported School Age Vacancies
columns.description: How many school age children a provider with an active referral agreement reportedly had room for when their referral agreement was last updated.

columns.name: Prequalified Prekindgarten Start Date
columns.description: The date a program first became qualified as a prekindergarten program under Act 166.

description: Vermont Child Care Provider Data including location, capacity, mailing list data and contact information, updated monthly. Data reflects the number of programs in business on the final day of the last complete month prior to the most recent update.

description: This health data inventory is the result of work conducted for the Vermont Health Care Innovation Project. While the goal of a Vermont Health Data Inventory is a unified data source for health care data, this project is just the first step in moving toward that goal. A unified data source would provide a gateway or portal to the diverse health data in Vermont. This includes many kinds of health related data: the all-payer claims dataset;health expenditures; clinical datasets; survey data; vital

name: Town Median Income 2014 All Returns
description: This report is provided for historical continuity with previous reports. Median AGI for all tax returns is not an inappropriate measure of central tendency because it is so dependent on the mix of single and dual (joint) incomes within a town. Determination of median AGI for all tax returns by town was required by VSA Title 16, Section 3480 in 1981 to assist in computation of aid to education. In 1987 this statute section was amended to specify determination of average AGI per exemption. The ent

description: Income and Tax statistics by town for calendar year 2014. Towns with fewer than ten returns have been aggregated into the row called "Suppressed."

name: Income Stats 2014 State By TI Bracket
description: This report shows income and tax statistics for all Vermont residents organized by Vermont taxable income bracket. Out-of-state filers were excluded from the analysis but their totals are reported on a separate line. Out-of-state filers with income adjsutment equal to zero (meaning they had no VT income) were excluded entirely.

columns.name: Appropriation Secondary Category
columns.description: A sub-category that the funding it associated with.

columns.name: Appropriation Primary Category
columns.description: A high level category that the funding is associated with.

columns.name: Appropriation ID
columns.description: The unique identifier associated with the funding.

columns.name: Appropriation Entity
columns.description: The entity responsible for managing and executing the funding.

columns.name: Amount
columns.description: The amount of funding dedicated for a particular purpose.

columns.name: Tier 1 Recipient
columns.description: The subsequent entity that receives the funding.

columns.name: Appropriation Purpose
columns.description: The specific purpose and intent of the funding.

columns.name: References
columns.description: Information helpful in understanding how the funding was initiated and changed over time.

name: Income Stats 2014 State By Decile
description: Income and Tax data organized by income (AGI) decile. Out-of-state filers are not included in decile analysis but totals are listed on a separate line. Out-of-state filers with income adjustments of 0 (meaning they had no Vermont income) were excluded entirely.

name: Income Stats 2014 - Under 65
description: This report looks at income and tax statistics for filers under the age of 65 organized by income (AGI) bracket. Out-of-state filers are excluded from the analysis but their totals are included on a separate line.

name: Income Stats 2014 State By AGI Bracket
description: This report shows income and tax statistics for all Vermont residents organized by AGI income bracket. Out-of-state filers were excluded from the analysis but their totals are reported on a separate line. Out-of-state filers with income adjsutment equal to zero (meaning they had no VT income) were excluded entirely.

name: 2015 Budget Data
description: Budget figures for the 2015 Fiscal Year for the State of Vermont by Department and Fund

name: Income Stats 2014 State - 65 And Over
description: This report looks at income and tax statistics for filers age 65 and up organized by income (AGI) bracket. Out-of-state filers are excluded from the analysis but their totals are included on a separate line.

description: The Federal Coronavirus Local Fiscal Recovery Fund (CLFRF) was established under section 9901 of the American Rescue Plan Act of 2021 (ARPA).The fund provides substantial funding to local governments. The U.S. Department of Treasury established two separate methods for local governments to request funding, depending on the type of local government. Local governments that are NOT metropolitan cities or counties – called non-entitlement units of local government (NEUs) – requested funds through th

name: Total Compensation and Expenses FY2025

columns.name: Overtime Pay
columns.description: This is cash paid for hours worked in excess of defined workday and/or workweek at rates established by law and/or in the applicable Collective Bargaining Agreements.

columns.name: Total Benefits
columns.description: State contribution to Retirement and Social Security, and where applicable Health, Dental, Life, Long Term Disability Insurance,  Family and Medical Leave Insurance, and Employee Assistance Program.

columns.name: Total Compensation
columns.description: The sum of "Total Pay," and "Total Benefits."

columns.name: Multiple
columns.description: When "Multiple Record" is indicated this means that there is more than one record for the same employee. This can be the result of that employee changing jobs, being employed in multiple temporary jobs, or moving from one job type to another (e.g., moving from a temporary job to a classified job).

columns.name: Total Expenses
columns.description: Includes reimbursement for mileage, meals and other expenses.

columns.name: Total Pay
columns.description: The sum of "Pay," "Other Pay," and "Overtime Pay."

columns.name: Overtime Units
columns.description: Units used to calculate overtime pay are based on premium and straight-time hours worked, shift differential and the like. Note: Overtime units cannot the used to determine overtime hours worked.

columns.name: Other Pay
columns.description: This is a category of payments made to the employee that are not "pay" or "overtime" and includes such items as shift differential, standby, clothing allowance, annual leave payoff (upon retirement or RIF), prior year compensatory leave payoff, etc.

columns.name: Pay
columns.description: The amount paid to the employee for "standard hours", which for most full-time employees is 2,080 hours per year (some protective service employees have standard hours greater than 2,080). This includes "regular" pay, paid holidays, sick leave, annual leave, compensatory leave, personal leave, and paid leave. Historically, temporary employees were paid only for hours worked - however, as of 2017 temporary employees are also paid for sick leave per 21 V.S.A. § 487. General Assembly members are paid a weekly rate during the legislative session.

columns.name: Job Type
columns.description: Classified, Exempt, General Assembly, Temporary, or Contractual

description: State employee salary, benefits and expense reimbursement information starting with fiscal year 2025. For the purpose of this report, all employees of the Department of States Attorneys and Sheriffs are included. Employees in the respective county offices, such as States Attorneys, Sheriffs and Deputy States Attorneys are County employees not State employees. To access the pay and expense data for Fiscal years 2009-2014, please see data set: Total Pay and Expenses FY2009-2014. To access the prev

description: All expenses related to the $1.25 billion in CRF funds received by the State of Vermont that were were authorized and expended in support of governmental entities, individuals, families, vulnerable populations and businesses affected by the 2020 coronavirus pandemic. Data is updated on a quarterly basis. Some data are suppressed due to legislative requirements. This data is not intended to be legal advice nor is it designed or intended to be relied upon as authoritative financial, investment, or

description: DISCLAIMER: This dataset may contain preliminary data that has not yet been verified and may be changed at a later date due to additional investigation. Additionally, the data entry process may include mechanical and/or human errors. Therefore, the Vermont State Police does not guarantee the accuracy, completeness, timeliness, or correct sequencing of the information. This dataset also excludes any records that would compromise the privacy of crime victims or the fidelity of ongoing investigatio

name: Town Property Tax Adjustment Summary
description: This file shows the total counts and amounts of property tax adjustments and renter rebates by town. The property tax adjustments were applied to households' FY16 property tax bills.

name: Violent Crime Rate - Statewide

columns.name: Year
columns.description: Calendar Year

columns.name: Violent Crime Rate
columns.description: Rate of Violent Crime Per 100,000 People

description: Rate of Violent Crime per 100,000 individuals, which includes the offenses of murder and nonnegligent manslaughter, rape (revised definition), robbery, and aggravated assault. Gathered through the National Incident Based Reporting System (NIBRS), Uniform Crime Reporting (UCR) program managed by the Federal Bureau of Investigation. Data is submitted by individual law enforcement agencies to the Department of Public Safety and forwarded for inclusion.

name: Vermonters Living in Poverty
provider: U.S. Census Bureau, American Community Survey

columns.name: Year
columns.description: The 12 month period during which income data was collected

columns.name: Percent of Vermonters Living below 200% FPL
columns.description: The percent of Vermont residents whose income status in the past 12-months is at or below 200% FPL

description: The percent of Vermont residents living at or below 200% of the Federal Poverty Level. The Federal Poverty Level is a measure of income issued every year by the Department of Health and Human Services (HHS). Federal poverty levels are used to determine eligibility for certain programs and benefits.

name: ARPA - State Fiscal Recovery Quarterly Project Overview

columns.name: Project Expenditure Category
columns.description: The expenditure category the project is associated with.  A project can only have one expenditure category.

columns.name: Entity
columns.description: The acronym associated with the unit responsible for reporting on the project.

columns.name: Project Description
columns.description: A brief description of the project.

columns.name: Total Expenditures
columns.description: The total amount ofARPA SFR dollars expended through this project as of reporting quarter end.  An expenditure is the amount that has been incurred as a liability of the entity (the  service has been rendered or the good has been delivered to the entity)

columns.name: Total Subaward Obligations
columns.description: The total amount of ARPA SFR dollars obligated for greater than or equal to $50,000.  Subawards include grants, contracts, and direct payments.

columns.name: Total Subaward Expenditures
columns.description: The total amount of ARPA SFR dollars expended for greater than or equal to $50,000.  Subawards include grants, contracts, and direct payments

columns.name: Total Aggregate Obligations
columns.description: The total amount of ARPA SFR dollars obligated for awards less than $50,000 and disbursements to individuals.

columns.name: Total Aggregate Expenditures
columns.description: The total amount of ARPA SFR dollars expended for awards less than $50,000 and disbursements to individuals.

columns.name: Number of Subawards
columns.description: The total number of subawards greater than or equal to $50,000.  Subawards include grants, contracts, and direct payments

columns.name: Project Identification Number
columns.description: The unique identification number associated with the project used for tracking purposes.

columns.name: Project Name
columns.description: The name of the project.

columns.name: Project Allocation
columns.description: The amount of ARPA SFR dollars allocated for the project.

columns.name: Total Obligations
columns.description: The total amount of ARPA SFR dollars obligated through this project as of reporting quarter end.  An obligation is an order placed for property and services, contracts and subawards made, and similar transactions that require payment

columns.name: Project Status
columns.description: The current status of the project.

columns.name: Project Expenditure Subcategory
columns.description: The expenditure subcategory the project is associated with.

description: As a recipient of American Rescue Plan Act (ARPA) State Fiscal Recovery Funds (SFR) the State of Vermont is required to submit quarterly Project and Expenditure Reports to the United States Treasury. Quarterly reports cover three calendar months and include various data elements including project, subaward and expenditure information. Treasury’s Portal for compliance reporting is organized by modules, each of which is a project component: project entry, subrecipient entry, subaward entry, and ex

description: A list of all State of Vermont component units. A governmental component unit is a legally separate organization for which the elected officials of the primary government are financially accountable. description: This data is drawn from Vermont's submissions to the Public Library Survey, administered by the Institute of Museum and Library Services (IMLS). The survey is intended to capture a statistical "year in the life" of a public library, and is intended to be filled out by every library in the country every year.

name: State of Vermont Employee Survey Engagement Score Summary by Year

columns.name: Supervisor
columns.description:
Supervisor – support, recognition and feedback questions on the Employee Engagement Survey
Q20.  My supervisor clearly explains my job performance expectations.  
Q21.  My supervisor regularly provides me with timely and useful feedback.  
Q22.   My supervisor gives me an opportunity to do my best work.  
Q23.  I am satisfied with the recognition I receive from my supervisor for my work.  
Q24.  My supervisor treats employees fairly and respectfully. Q25.  My supervisor seems to care about me as a person.

columns.name: Balance
columns.description:
Balance – work-life questions on the Employee Engagement Survey
Q33.  The amount of work I am expected to perform is reasonable. Q34.  My job allows a good balance between work and my personal life

columns.name: Satisfaction
columns.description:
Satisfaction – work and employer questions from the Employee Engagement Survey
Q4.  I enjoy performing the day-to-day work of my job. 
Q5.  The work I perform is meaningful and rewarding. 
Q6.    I would recommend the State of Vermont to others as a great place to work 
Q41.  I feel that working for the State of Vermont provides me with a solid career path. 
Q45.  In general, I am satisfied with my job.

columns.name: Year
columns.description: Represents the year the Employee Engagement Survey was taken by State of Vermont Executive Branch Classified and Exempt employees

columns.name: Engagement Score Summary
columns.description: The overall engagement score is an average of the overall data from the seven component question sets - Growth, Alignment, Supervisor, Peers, Balance, Communication and Satisfaction

columns.name: Growth
columns.description:
Growth – personal growth and development questions on Employee Engagement Survey
Q26.  My supervisor provides the help I need to improve my job performance. 
Q27.  I have an opportunity to learn and grow professionally  
Q28.  I receive the training I need to perform my job.  
Q29.  My supervisor and I discuss and plan my career development.

columns.name: Alignment
columns.description:
Alignment – understanding the link between one’s job and the organization’s mission questions on the Employee Engagement Survey
Q1.  I understand my job duties and responsibilities. 
Q2.   I understand the work, goals, and mission of my department or agency.  
Q3.  The work I perform is linked to my department or agency meeting its goals and mission

columns.name: Peers
columns.description:
Peers – positive relationships in the workplace questions on the Employee Engagement Survey
Q12.  The people I work with treat each other respectfully.  
Q13.  The people I work with care about me.  
Q14.  My fellow employees are committed to doing good work.  Q15.  The employees in my work group work well together as a team.

columns.name: Communication
columns.description:
Communication – value employee voices, ideas, opinions questions on the Employee Engagement Survey
Q7.   Management and senior leadership communicates important information effectively. 
Q8.   I have an opportunity to provide feedback to management and senior leadership. 
Q9.  I am encouraged to share ideas on improving either service delivery or business process efficiency. 
Q16.  I feel I can communicate honestly and openly in my workplace.

description: The employee engagement survey is distributed to all classified and exempt employees in the Executive Branch annually. Survey participation is voluntary, and the scores only reflect those who participated in the process. An “Employee Engagement Score” is calculated as an index of overall employee engagement. The index is the average of seven components of employee engagement, each based on a subset of questions in the employee engagement survey: Growth – personal growth and development, Balance

name: Unhoused Vermonters
provider: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development

columns.name: Year
columns.description: The year during which the annual Point in Time count took place.

columns.name: Number of Vermonters
columns.description: The number of Vermonters counted as being homeless on the night of the annual Point in Time count.

description: Number of adults and children experiencing literal homelessness as captured in the annual Point-in-Time count. Literal homelessness is defined as staying in emergency shelter, transitional housing for homeless persons, a place not meant for human habitation, or a motel with a voucher. The count for 2022 is based on preliminary data from Vermont Coalition to End Homelessness and is subject to change.
